L1.area: REST API request handling and database query construction layer

L2.description: A N1QL injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled parameters from a REST API endpoint are interpolated directly into N1QL query strings without parameterization, allowing an attacker to insert additional query statements.

L3.area: N1QL query construction for document listing and access control queries
L3.description: A N1QL injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled key range and user identity parameters from the document listing REST API endpoint are interpolated directly into N1QL query strings without parameterization, allowing an attacker to insert additional N1QL statements.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled startkey, endkey, and username parameters are interpolated directly into N1QL query strings via string formatting without parameterization, enabling N1QL injection attacks.
